The automatic guided trolley can stop sa.goal: The automatic guided trolley can stop safely when encountering obstacles.
The automatic guided trolley can stop sa.strategy: The safe stopping of the trolley can be determined by examining its sensor system and control algorithm.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system can detect obstacles accurately.
subgoals.G1.2: The control algorithm can initiate safe stopping maneuvers.

solutions.Sn1.1: Sensor calibration reports, obstacle detection test results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Algorithm verification results, simulation of safe stopping maneuvers.

The sensor system can detect obstacles a.goal: The sensor system can detect obstacles accurately.
The sensor system can detect obstacles a.strategy: The accuracy of the sensor system can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor hardware is calibrated and functioning properly.
subgoals.G1.2: The sensor software algorithms are accurate and reliable.

solutions.Sn1.1: Calibration test results, sensor maintenance records.
solutions.Sn1.2: Algorithm validation reports, sensor performance evaluations.

The control algorithm can initiate safe.goal: The control algorithm can initiate safe stopping maneuvers.
The control algorithm can initiate safe.strategy: The control algorithm's ability to initiate safe stopping maneuvers can be determined by examining its inputs, logic, and outputs.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm receives accurate sensor inputs.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm incorporates logic to detect emergency situations.
subgoals.G1.3: The algorithm outputs commands for safe stopping maneuvers.

solutions.Sn1.1: Sensor calibration reports, sensor accuracy tests.
solutions.Sn1.2: Algorithm logic analysis, emergency situation detection tests.
solutions.Sn1.3: Verification and validation of output commands.

The sensor hardware is calibrated and fu.goal: The sensor hardware is calibrated and functioning properly.
The sensor hardware is calibrated and fu.strategy: The calibration and functioning of the sensor hardware can be assessed by examining its individual components.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor components are calibrated accurately.
subgoals.G1.2: The sensor components are functioning according to specifications.

solutions.Sn1.1: Calibration reports, manufacturer documentation.
solutions.Sn1.2: Functional testing results, performance benchmarks.

The sensor software algorithms are accur.goal: The sensor software algorithms are accurate and reliable.
The sensor software algorithms are accur.strategy: The accuracy and reliability of the sensor software algorithms can be determined by examining the algorithm design, input validation, and error handling mechanisms.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm design is robust and error-free.
subgoals.G1.2: The input validation mechanism effectively filters out invalid sensor data.
subgoals.G1.3: The error handling mechanisms handle unexpected situations appropriately.

solutions.Sn1.1: Algorithm design documentation, code reviews.
solutions.Sn1.2: Input validation test cases, input validation results.
solutions.Sn1.3: Error handling test scenarios, error handling results.

The algorithm receives accurate sensor i.goal: The algorithm receives accurate sensor inputs.
The algorithm receives accurate sensor i.strategy: The accuracy of the sensor inputs can be determined by examining the sensor calibration and data processing.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensors are properly calibrated.
subgoals.G1.2: The data processing algorithms accurately interpret sensor data.

solutions.Sn1.1: Calibration reports, sensor validation tests.
solutions.Sn1.2: Data validation tests, comparison with ground truth data.

The algorithm incorporates logic to dete.goal: The algorithm incorporates logic to detect emergency situations.
The algorithm incorporates logic to dete.strategy: The algorithm's ability to detect emergency situations can be assessed by examining its logic and decision-making process.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm has rules and conditions to identify emergency situations.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm can differentiate between emergency and non-emergency situations.

solutions.Sn1.1: Code analysis, documentation of emergency detection rules.
solutions.Sn1.2: Simulation tests, real-world scenario evaluations.

The algorithm outputs commands for safe.goal: The algorithm outputs commands for safe stopping maneuvers.
The algorithm outputs commands for safe.strategy: The safety of the stopping maneuvers can be determined by examining the algorithm's inputs, calculations, and outputs.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm receives accurate and reliable sensor data.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm calculates the appropriate stopping distance and time.
subgoals.G1.3: The algorithm outputs the correct commands for safe stopping.

solutions.Sn1.1: Sensor calibration reports, data validation techniques.
solutions.Sn1.2: Mathematical models, simulation results.
solutions.Sn1.3: Test scenarios, validation against known safe stopping maneuvers.

The sensor components are calibrated acc.goal: The sensor components are calibrated accurately.
The sensor components are calibrated acc.strategy: The accuracy of sensor calibration can be determined by examining the calibration procedure and the calibration results.

subgoals.G1.1: The calibration procedure is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The sensor outputs are within acceptable ranges after calibration.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer calibration guidelines, calibration reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Sensor output measurements, comparison with reference standards.

The sensor components are functioning ac.goal: The sensor components are functioning according to specifications.
The sensor components are functioning ac.strategy: The functionality of sensor components can be determined by examining their calibration, accuracy, and response time.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensors are properly calibrated.
subgoals.G1.2: The sensors provide accurate measurements.
subgoals.G1.3: The sensors have an acceptable response time.

solutions.Sn1.1: Calibration reports, calibration verification tests.
solutions.Sn1.2: Comparison with reference measurements, accuracy assessments.
solutions.Sn1.3: Performance tests, benchmarking against response time requirements.

The algorithm design is robust and error.goal: The algorithm design is robust and error-free.
The algorithm design is robust and error.strategy: The robustness and error-freeness of the algorithm design can be determined by examining its input validation, error handling, and testing procedures.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm design includes comprehensive input validation.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm design includes robust error handling mechanisms.
subgoals.G1.3: The algorithm design has been thoroughly tested for errors.

solutions.Sn1.1: Code review reports, documentation on input validation techniques used.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code review reports, documentation on error handling mechanisms implemented.
solutions.Sn1.3: Test reports, test coverage metrics.

The input validation mechanism effective.goal: The input validation mechanism effectively filters out invalid sensor data.
The input validation mechanism effective.strategy: The input validation mechanism can be evaluated based on its ability to filter out different types of invalid sensor data.

subgoals.G1.1: The mechanism filters out data with values outside the valid range.
subgoals.G1.2: The mechanism filters out data with inconsistent timestamp information.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test scenarios with data outside the valid range, results showing filtered out data.
solutions.Sn1.2: Test scenarios with data having inconsistent timestamps, results showing filtered out data.

The error handling mechanisms handle une.goal: The error handling mechanisms handle unexpected situations appropriately.
The error handling mechanisms handle une.strategy: The error handling mechanisms can be evaluated by examining their response to different types of unexpected situations.

subgoals.G1.1: The error handling mechanisms respond appropriately to input validation errors.
subgoals.G1.2: The error handling mechanisms respond appropriately to database connection errors.
subgoals.G1.3: The error handling mechanisms respond appropriately to server timeout errors.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test cases with invalid input, code reviews.
solutions.Sn1.2: Simulated database connection errors, error logs.
solutions.Sn1.3: Simulated server timeouts, performance testing results.

The sensors are properly calibrated..strategy: Proper calibration means that the sensors are adjusted to accurately measure the desired parameters.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensors provide accurate measurements.
subgoals.G1.2: The calibration process is performed correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Calibration test results, comparison with reference measurements.
solutions.Sn1.2: Calibration procedure documentation, training records for calibration technicians.

The data processing algorithms accuratel.goal: The data processing algorithms accurately interpret sensor data.
The data processing algorithms accuratel.strategy: The accuracy of the data processing algorithms can be determined by examining their individual components and performance.

subgoals.G1.1: The preprocessing stage correctly filters and normalizes sensor data.
subgoals.G1.2: The feature extraction stage accurately identifies relevant features from the preprocessed data.
subgoals.G1.3: The classification stage correctly classifies the extracted features.

solutions.Sn1.1: Preprocessing algorithm validation, data consistency checks.
solutions.Sn1.2: Feature extraction algorithm validation, comparison with ground truth data.
solutions.Sn1.3: Classification algorithm validation, comparison with labeled data.

The algorithm has rules and conditions t.goal: The algorithm has rules and conditions to identify emergency situations.
The algorithm has rules and conditions t.strategy: The algorithm's ability to identify emergency situations can be determined by examining its rules and conditions.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm has rules to detect abnormal behavior.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm has conditions to distinguish emergency situations from regular situations.

solutions.Sn1.1: Algorithm documentation, code review reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Test scenarios, simulation results.

The algorithm can differentiate between.goal: The algorithm can differentiate between emergency and non-emergency situations.
The algorithm can differentiate between.strategy: The algorithm's ability to differentiate between emergency and non-emergency situations can be evaluated based on its accuracy, precision, and recall.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm has high accuracy in identifying emergency situations.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm has high precision in identifying emergency situations.
subgoals.G1.3: The algorithm has high recall in identifying emergency situations.

solutions.Sn1.1: Testing the algorithm on a dataset with known emergency and non-emergency situations and measuring accuracy.
solutions.Sn1.2: Testing the algorithm on a dataset with known emergency situations and measuring precision.
solutions.Sn1.3: Testing the algorithm on a dataset with known emergency situations and measuring recall.

The algorithm receives accurate and reli.goal: The algorithm receives accurate and reliable sensor data.
The algorithm receives accurate and reli.strategy: The accuracy and reliability of the sensor data can be determined by examining the sensor calibration and data validation processes.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor calibration process is effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The data validation process is robust.

solutions.Sn1.1: Calibration reports, sensor calibration test results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Validation reports, data validation test results.

The algorithm calculates the appropriate.goal: The algorithm calculates the appropriate stopping distance and time.
The algorithm calculates the appropriate.strategy: The algorithm's calculation of stopping distance and time can be evaluated by examining its inputs, calculations, and outputs.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm takes into account the speed of the vehicle.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm considers the road conditions.
subgoals.G1.3: The algorithm incorporates the braking capabilities of the vehicle.

solutions.Sn1.1: Algorithm documentation, code reviews.
solutions.Sn1.2: Data from speed sensors, road condition monitoring systems.
solutions.Sn1.3: Vehicle braking system specifications, braking performance tests.

The algorithm outputs the correct comman.goal: The algorithm outputs the correct commands for safe stopping.
The algorithm outputs the correct comman.strategy: The correctness of the algorithm can be determined by examining its input, output, and logic.

subgoals.G1.1: The input to the algorithm is accurate.
subgoals.G1.2: The output of the algorithm results in safe stopping.
subgoals.G1.3: The logic of the algorithm is free from errors.

solutions.Sn1.1: Sensor data validation, input verification techniques.
solutions.Sn1.2: Simulation results, real-world testing.
solutions.Sn1.3: Code reviews, formal verification techniques.

RTK HERE+ provides accuracy within 5 cms.goal: RTK HERE+ provides accuracy within 5 cms.
RTK HERE+ provides accuracy within 5 cms.strategy: The accuracy of RTK HERE+ can be determined by examining its performance characteristics.

subgoals.G1.1: RTK HERE+ achieves centimeter-level positioning accuracy.

solutions.Sn1.1: Datasheets, performance specifications, independent evaluations.

RTK functions correctly on America hexco.goal: RTK functions correctly on America hexcopters with S-900 folding wings airframe.
RTK functions correctly on America hexco.strategy: The functionality of RTK on America hexcopters with S-900 folding wings airframe can be determined by examining the hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The RTK hardware is properly installed and calibrated.
subgoals.G1.2: The software algorithm for RTK is accurate and reliable.

solutions.Sn1.1: Installation and calibration reports for the RTK hardware.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code audits for the RTK software algorithm.

UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.goal: UAVs flying in the managed airspace will not collide with fixed ground-based obstacles (FGB)
UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.strategy: The goal can be achieved by ensuring the UAVs have obstacle detection and avoidance capabilities.

subgoals.G1.1: The UAV's obstacle detection system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The UAV's obstacle avoidance algorithm is effective.
subgoals.G1.3: The UAV's communication system is reliable to receive obstacle information from the airspace management system.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer test reports, third-party evaluations.
solutions.Sn1.2: Simulation results, flight testing records.
solutions.Sn1.3: Communication logs, system performance test results.

Comply to ISAC requirements..strategy: Compliance to ISAC requirements can be achieved by addressing different aspects of the requirements.

subgoals.G1.1: Implementing access controls.
subgoals.G1.2: Implementing encryption of sensitive data.
subgoals.G1.3: Implementing regular vulnerability assessments.

solutions.Sn1.1: Access control policies, monitoring logs.
solutions.Sn1.2: Encryption protocols, data protection measures.
solutions.Sn1.3: Vulnerability assessment reports, patch management records.

UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.goal: UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based objects.
UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.strategy: The avoidance of fixed Ground-Based objects can be achieved by analyzing the UAV's sensor system and navigation algorithm.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system can detect and accurately locate fixed Ground-Based objects.
subgoals.G1.2: The navigation algorithm can calculate and execute appropriate avoidance maneuvers.

solutions.Sn1.1: Sensor calibration reports, obstacle detection and location accuracy tests.
solutions.Sn1.2: Navigation algorithm verification and validation, simulation results.

Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.goal: Comply to infrastructure safety case (ISAC) requirements.
Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.strategy: The compliance to ISAC requirements can be determined by examining various aspects of the infrastructure safety case.

subgoals.G1.1: The identification and analysis of safety hazards in the infrastructure.
subgoals.G1.2: The implementation and effectiveness of safety measures.
subgoals.G1.3: The documentation and verification of safety-related activities.

solutions.Sn1.1: Hazard identification reports, risk assessments.
solutions.Sn1.2: Inspection records, maintenance logs.
solutions.Sn1.3: Safety plans, test results.

Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.goal: Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) provides correct instructions for avoiding collisions between two UAVs.
Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.strategy: The correctness of the Collision Avoidance Algorithm can be determined by examining its inputs, outputs, and the logic used in determining collision avoidance instructions.

subgoals.G1.1: The inputs to the Collision Avoidance Algorithm are accurate.
subgoals.G1.2: The outputs of the Collision Avoidance Algorithm are correct.
subgoals.G1.3: The logic used in determining collision avoidance instructions is sound.

solutions.Sn1.1: Sensor data verification, calibration reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Simulation results, real-world testing.
solutions.Sn1.3: Algorithm code review, verification of mathematical models used in the algorithm.

GPS is accurate within 5 cms..strategy: The accuracy of GPS can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The GPS receiver is precise.
subgoals.G1.2: The satellite positioning system is accurate.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer specifications, third-party evaluations.
solutions.Sn1.2: Signal quality analysis, satellite ephemeris data.

The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.goal: The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery is sufficiently mitigated.
The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.strategy: The hazard of running out of battery can be mitigated by ensuring a reliable battery life and implementing effective low battery warning system.

subgoals.G1.1: The battery life is sufficient for the intended operation.
subgoals.G1.2: The low battery warning system is effective.

solutions.Sn1.1: Battery performance tests, manufacturer specifications.
solutions.Sn1.2: User feedback on warning system, testing of warning system functionality.

All identified causes for the hazard, El.goal: All identified causes for the hazard, El1, are sufficiently managed.
All identified causes for the hazard, El.strategy: The hazard, El1, can be managed by addressing its identified causes.

subgoals.G1.1: Identify all causes for hazard El1.
subgoals.G1.2: Develop strategies to manage each identified cause.

solutions.Sn1.1: Hazard analysis reports, incident reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Risk management plans, mitigation strategies.

The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.goal: The applied mitigations lead to sufficient risk reduction of the hazard El1.
The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.strategy: The applied mitigations are equivalent to the required risk reduction for hazard El1.

subgoals.G1.1: The required risk reduction for hazard El1 is achieved.

solutions.Sn1.1: Risk assessment reports, safety analysis showing equivalence of applied mitigations and required risk reduction.

The mitigation requirement R1 holds.strategy: The mitigation requirement R1 can be evaluated by examining its components and verifying their effectiveness.

subgoals.G1.1: The first component of R1 is effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The second component of R1 is effective.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test results, analysis reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Expert assessments, historical data.

Contract-based formal verification shows.goal: Contract-based formal verification shows with sufficient confidence that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover.
Contract-based formal verification shows.strategy: Contract-based formal verification can provide evidence that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover.

subgoals.G1.1: The contract-based formal verification process is reliable and accurate.
subgoals.G1.2: The contract specifies the desired property R1.
subgoals.G1.3: The contract-based formal verification process successfully verifies the achievement of property R1.

solutions.Sn1.1: Historical records of successful verification using contract-based formal verification.
solutions.Sn1.2: Documentation showing the specification of property R1 in the contract.
solutions.Sn1.3: Verification reports demonstrating the successful verification of property R1.

System and its elements are designed to.goal: System and its elements are designed to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses (radiation) for the life of the mission (NASA R&M).
System and its elements are designed to.strategy: The goal can be supported by examining the system's design and its ability to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses, including radiation.

subgoals.G1.1: The system design incorporates redundancy and fault-tolerant features.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has been tested and validated under extreme load and stress conditions, including radiation.

solutions.Sn1.1: Design documentation, fault tolerance analysis.
solutions.Sn1.2: Test reports, radiation testing results.

System is tolerant to radiation-induced.goal: System is tolerant to radiation-induced faults and failures (NASA R&M mod).
System is tolerant to radiation-induced.strategy: The system's tolerance to radiation-induced faults and failures can be determined by examining its hardware components and calculating the probability of failure under radiation.

subgoals.G1.1: The hardware components are radiation-hardened.
subgoals.G1.2: The probability of failure under radiation is within acceptable limits.

solutions.Sn1.1: Radiation hardening techniques used in hardware design, manufacturer specifications.
solutions.Sn1.2: Radiation testing reports, probabilistic analysis of failure rates under radiation.

System is able to recover from anomalies.goal: System is able to recover from anomalies affecting functions that are important to top-level expectations (NASA R&M).
System is able to recover from anomalies.strategy: The system's ability to recover can be evaluated by examining its anomaly detection and response mechanisms.

subgoals.G1.1: The system can detect anomalies affecting important functions.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can respond to anomalies and recover the affected functions.

solutions.Sn1.1: Anomaly detection algorithms, system logs.
solutions.Sn1.2: Recovery mechanisms documentation, system test results.

COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.goal: COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requirement: No latch-up seen up to 5e9 protons/cm2 for 200 MeV protons.
COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.strategy: The COTS parts' ability to pass the mission proton SEL requirement can be determined by examining their latch-up behavior under specified proton fluence and energy conditions.

subgoals.G1.1: The latch-up behavior of COTS parts under proton fluence of 5e9 protons/cm2.
subgoals.G1.2: The latch-up behavior of COTS parts under 200 MeV proton energy.

solutions.Sn1.1: Latch-up test reports for COTS parts under 5e9 protons/cm2.
solutions.Sn1.2: Latch-up test reports for COTS parts under 200 MeV proton energy.

System remains functional for intended r.goal: System remains functional for intended radiation environment(NASA R&M mod) in order to complete science mission objective: Record the number of upsets in 28nm bulk SRAM in LEO for a period of 1 year.
System remains functional for intended r.strategy: The system's functionality in the intended radiation environment can be determined by examining its hardware components and incorporating evidence of successful operation in similar radiation environments.

subgoals.G1.1: The system's hardware components are radiation hardened.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has been successfully operated in similar radiation environments.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer specifications for radiation hardening, radiation test reports for individual components.
solutions.Sn1.2: Records of successful mission operations in similar radiation environments, radiation test reports for similar systems.

We see a world where everyone who choose.goal: We see a world where everyone who chooses to use these services, as well as those who do not, have confidence in a safe and secure sector.
We see a world where everyone who choose.strategy: The confidence in a safe and secure sector can be achieved by ensuring the safety and security of the services and effectively communicating this to the users.

subgoals.G1.1: The services are safe and secure.
subgoals.G1.2: Effective communication about safety and security measures.

solutions.Sn1.1: Comprehensive risk assessments, security audits, user feedback.
solutions.Sn1.2: Clear and transparent communication about safety and security measures through user agreements, privacy policies, and public awareness campaigns.

There is justified confidence that cyber.goal: There is justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.
There is justified confidence that cyber.strategy: Cyber-security measures are implemented and tested to ensure the safety and resilience of UK regulated services.

subgoals.G1.1: Cyber-security measures are in place.
subgoals.G1.2: The effectiveness of cyber-security measures is verified.

solutions.Sn1.1: Cyber-security policies and procedures, security audits.
solutions.Sn1.2: Penetration testing, vulnerability assessments.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The justification of confidence can be achieved by examining the cyber-security measures and their effectiveness in mitigating risks.

subgoals.G1.1: The cyber-security measures are robust.
subgoals.G1.2: The risks posed by cyber-security issues are acceptable.
subgoals.G1.3: The regulated services are resilient to cyber-security threats.

solutions.Sn1.1: Penetration testing results, security audits.
solutions.Sn1.2: Risk assessments, threat modeling.
solutions.Sn1.3: Business continuity plans, incident response plans.

Other stakeholders have justified confid.goal: Other stakeholders have justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.
Other stakeholders have justified confid.strategy: The confidence of stakeholders can be achieved by addressing the safety and resilience aspects of the UK regulated services.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety measures in place are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The resilience measures in place are effective.

solutions.Sn1.1: Safety audits, incident response testing.
solutions.Sn1.2: Resilience testing, disaster recovery plans.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services at present.
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The cyber-security of UK regulated services can be assessed by examining the safety and resilience of their systems and evaluating the risks posed by cyber-security issues.

subgoals.G1.1: The systems of UK regulated services are safe and resilient.
subgoals.G1.2: The risks posed by cyber-security issues are acceptable.

solutions.Sn1.1: System audits, penetration testing, incident response records.
solutions.Sn1.2: Risk assessments, vulnerability assessments, threat intelligence reports.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues will not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services in the future.
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be determined by examining the measures in place for preventing and mitigating cyber attacks.

subgoals.G1.1: The preventive measures are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The mitigative measures are effective.

solutions.Sn1.1: Penetration testing reports, vulnerability assessments.
solutions.Sn1.2: Incident response reports, disaster recovery plans.

ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.goal: ARO regulatory activity provides justified confidence in the security and resilience.
ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.strategy: Regulatory activity ensures compliance with security and resilience standards.

subgoals.G1.1: The regulatory standards for security and resilience are met.

solutions.Sn1.1: Regulatory compliance reports, audit results.

ARO has justified confidence in the over.goal: ARO has justified confidence in the overall regulatory institutions and standards.
ARO has justified confidence in the over.strategy: The confidence in regulatory institutions and standards can be determined by examining their reliability and effectiveness.

subgoals.G1.1: The regulatory institutions are reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The regulatory standards are effective.

solutions.Sn1.1: Historical records of regulatory institution performance, third-party assessments.
solutions.Sn1.2: Compliance rates with regulatory standards, safety records.

ARO has justified confidence that the re.goal: ARO has justified confidence that the regulated system can deal with cyber related events and changes.
ARO has justified confidence that the re.strategy: The justified confidence in the regulated system can be achieved by examining its ability to handle cyber related events and changes.

subgoals.G1.1: The system has robust cybersecurity measures.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can effectively detect and respond to cyber threats.
subgoals.G1.3: The system can adapt to changes in the cyber landscape.

solutions.Sn1.1: Security audit reports, penetration testing results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Incident response documentation, intrusion detection system reports.
solutions.Sn1.3: Change management records, vulnerability assessments.

ARO has justified confidence in the tech.goal: ARO has justified confidence in the technical approaches for addressing cyber-security informed safety and resilience.
ARO has justified confidence in the tech.strategy: The technical approaches can be evaluated by examining their effectiveness in addressing cyber-security, safety, and resilience.

subgoals.G1.1: The technical approaches effectively address cyber-security.
subgoals.G1.2: The technical approaches effectively address safety.
subgoals.G1.3: The technical approaches effectively address resilience.

solutions.Sn1.1: Penetration testing reports, vulnerability assessments.
solutions.Sn1.2: Safety analysis reports, hazard identification and assessment.
solutions.Sn1.3: Resilience testing reports, risk assessments.

ARO provides other stakeholders with jus.goal: ARO provides other stakeholders with justified confidence in the regulated security and resilience.
ARO provides other stakeholders with jus.strategy: The confidence in ARO can be determined by examining its security features and resilience capabilities.

subgoals.G1.1: The security features of ARO are robust.
subgoals.G1.2: The resilience capabilities of ARO are effective.

solutions.Sn1.1: Security audits, penetration testing.
solutions.Sn1.2: Disaster recovery plans, incident response exercises.

Provides confidence existing system secu.goal: Provides confidence existing system secure and resilient

subgoals.G1.1: The system has implemented strong access control measures.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has a robust backup and recovery plan.
subgoals.G1.3: The system has undergone penetration tests to identify vulnerabilities.

solutions.Sn1.1: Security logs, access control policy documentation.

solutions.Sn1.3: Penetration test reports, vulnerability assessment reports.

Sources of doubt actively managed - actu.goal: Sources of doubt actively managed - actual and perceived risks.
Sources of doubt actively managed - actu.strategy: The sources of doubt can be divided into actual risks and perceived risks.

subgoals.G1.1: Actual risks are identified and mitigated.
subgoals.G1.2: Perceived risks are addressed through communication and transparency.

solutions.Sn1.1: Risk assessment reports, risk mitigation strategies.
solutions.Sn1.2: Clear communication channels, regular updates to stakeholders.

The vehicle is acceptably safe.strategy: The safety of the vehicle can be determined by examining its structural integrity, braking system, and crash avoidance features.

subgoals.G1.1: The vehicle's structural integrity meets safety standards.
subgoals.G1.2: The braking system is reliable and effective.
subgoals.G1.3: The crash avoidance features function properly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Crash test results, structural integrity analysis.
solutions.Sn1.2: Braking system performance tests, manufacturer test reports.
solutions.Sn1.3: Crash avoidance system evaluations, real-world incident data.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements during product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The argument can be broken down into sub-arguments that focus on different aspects of safety requirements implementation.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety requirements are clearly defined.
subgoals.G1.2: The safety requirements are properly implemented.
subgoals.G1.3: The safety requirements are adequately tested.

solutions.Sn1.1: Safety requirement documentation, industry standards.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code reviews, inspections, verification and validation processes.
solutions.Sn1.3: Test plans, test results, safety audits.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements post product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The argument can be decomposed into sub-arguments related to different aspects of safety requirements implementation.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety requirements are clearly defined.
subgoals.G1.2: The safety requirements are correctly implemented.
subgoals.G1.3: The safety requirements are effectively enforced.

solutions.Sn1.1: Safety requirement documentation, regulatory standards.
solutions.Sn1.2: Inspection reports, quality assurance processes.
solutions.Sn1.3: Compliance audits, regular safety assessments.

The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.goal: The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Safety Requirements
The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.strategy: The safety of the vehicle can be determined by examining its adherence to pre-defined safety requirements.

subgoals.G1.1: The vehicle's braking system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The vehicle's stability control system functions correctly.
subgoals.G1.3: The vehicle's airbag system operates as intended.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer test reports, third-party evaluations.
solutions.Sn1.2: Vehicle performance simulations.
solutions.Sn1.3: Crash tests, safety certification reports.

System is acceptably safe to operate in.goal: System is acceptably safe to operate in the specified environment
System is acceptably safe to operate in.strategy: The safety of the system can be determined by examining its hardware, software, and operational procedures.

subgoals.G1.1: The hardware components are reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The software components are secure.
subgoals.G1.3: The operational procedures are followed correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer test reports, third-party evaluations.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code audits, vulnerability scans.
solutions.Sn1.3: Training records, compliance audits.

The residual risk associated with the id.goal: The residual risk associated with the identified system hazards has been reduced to an acceptable level.
The residual risk associated with the id.strategy: The residual risk can be reduced by addressing individual system hazards and implementing suitable risk mitigation measures.

subgoals.G1.1: Identify and assess system hazards.
subgoals.G1.2: Implement risk mitigation measures for each identified hazard.
subgoals.G1.3: Ensure the residual risk is reduced to an acceptable level.

solutions.Sn1.1: Hazard analysis reports, risk assessments.
solutions.Sn1.2: Risk mitigation plans, safety procedures.
solutions.Sn1.3: Verification and validation activities, compliance with safety standards.

Functional Safety: The residual risk of.goal: Functional Safety: The residual risk of (hazard T) is acceptable
Functional Safety: The residual risk of.strategy: The acceptability of the residual risk can be determined by examining the severity and probability of hazard T, as well as the effectiveness of safety measures.

subgoals.G1.1: The severity of hazard T is within acceptable limits.
subgoals.G1.2: The probability of hazard T occurring is within acceptable limits.
subgoals.G1.3: The safety measures implemented effectively mitigate the risk of hazard T.

solutions.Sn1.1: Risk assessment reports, hazard analysis.
solutions.Sn1.2: Probability calculations, historical data.
solutions.Sn1.3: Safety validation test results, safety certification documentation.

Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goa.goal: Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goal U has reduced the occurrence or propagation of hazard T causes
Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goa.strategy: The reduction in occurrence or propagation of hazard T can be determined by examining the implementation of Safety Goal U and its effects.

subgoals.G1.1: Safety Goal U has been implemented.
subgoals.G1.2: The occurrence of hazard T has reduced.
subgoals.G1.3: The propagation of hazard T has reduced.

solutions.Sn1.1: Documentation of the implementation of Safety Goal U.
solutions.Sn1.2: Data showing a decrease in the occurrence of hazard T.
solutions.Sn1.3: Data showing a decrease in the propagation of hazard T.

Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by a.goal: Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by appeal to detection and diagnostics of systematic and random failures within the design.
Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by a.strategy: The detection and diagnostics of systematic and random failures can be achieved through comprehensive testing and monitoring.

subgoals.G1.1: Comprehensive testing is conducted to detect systematic failures.
subgoals.G1.2: Monitoring systems are in place to detect random failures.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test plans, test results, test coverage metrics.
solutions.Sn1.2: Monitoring logs, system health reports.

System Degradation: Argument by appeal o.goal: System Degradation: Argument by appeal over the transition to a safe system state in an appropriate time
System Degradation: Argument by appeal o.strategy: The system degradation can be evaluated by examining the time taken to transition to a safe state during failures.

subgoals.G1.1: The system can detect failures within a reasonable time.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can initiate a safe state transition within an appropriate time.

solutions.Sn1.1: Failure detection logs, system monitoring data.
solutions.Sn1.2: Safe state transition logs, system response time analysis.

Random Failures: The impact of random fa.goal: Random Failures: The impact of random failures which can invalidate the Safety Goal U have been adequately managed
Random Failures: The impact of random fa.strategy: The impact of random failures can be determined by analyzing the system's fault tolerance measures and mitigation strategies.

subgoals.G1.1: The system has fault tolerance mechanisms in place.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has effective mitigation strategies for random failures.

solutions.Sn1.1: System documentation, design specifications showing fault tolerance mechanisms.
solutions.Sn1.2: Risk assessments, contingency plans showing mitigation strategies.

A justified system safe state has been d.goal: A justified system safe state has been developed.
A justified system safe state has been d.strategy: The justified system safe state can be achieved by ensuring the correctness of system components and their interactions.

subgoals.G1.1: The system components are functioning correctly.
subgoals.G1.2: The interactions between system components are reliable.

solutions.Sn1.1: Component test reports, verification and validation results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Documentation of system architecture, analysis of system behavior.

Restriction: Vehicle functionality has b.goal: Restriction: Vehicle functionality has been restricted
Restriction: Vehicle functionality has b.strategy: The restriction of vehicle functionality can be examined by analyzing specific components and features.

subgoals.G1.1: The speed of the vehicle is limited.
subgoals.G1.2: The acceleration of the vehicle is limited.
subgoals.G1.3: The access to certain features is restricted.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer test reports, speed limit control system documentation.
solutions.Sn1.2: Manufacturer test reports, acceleration control system documentation.
solutions.Sn1.3: Documentation on feature access control mechanisms.

The implemented pacemaker software is ac.goal: The implemented pacemaker software is acceptably safe to operate in VVI mode.
The implemented pacemaker software is ac.strategy: The safety of the pacemaker software can be determined by examining its functionality and performance in VVI mode.

subgoals.G1.1: The software accurately detects ventricular events.
subgoals.G1.2: The software properly adjusts pacing mode.
subgoals.G1.3: The software is resilient to external interference.

solutions.Sn1.1: Verification test reports, clinical studies.
solutions.Sn1.2: Software analysis, simulation results.
solutions.Sn1.3: Interference testing, system robustness evaluation.

The implementation satisfies all the des.goal: The implementation satisfies all the desired safety properties within acceptable timing tolerances.
The implementation satisfies all the des.strategy: The safety properties can be decomposed into individual properties and each property can be evaluated for compliance within acceptable timing tolerances.

subgoals.G1.1: All safety properties are identified.
subgoals.G1.2: Each safety property is evaluated for compliance within acceptable timing tolerances.

solutions.Sn1.1: Safety property identification documentation, safety analysis reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Timing analysis reports, performance testing results.

The model satisfies all the desired prop.goal: The model satisfies all the desired properties.
The model satisfies all the desired prop.strategy: The desired properties can be broken down into individual sub-properties and each sub-property can be evaluated separately.

subgoals.G1.1: Sub-property 1 is satisfied.
subgoals.G1.2: Sub-property 2 is satisfied.
subgoals.G1.3: Sub-property 3 is satisfied.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test results showing satisfaction of sub-property 1.
solutions.Sn1.2: Validation data confirming satisfaction of sub-property 2.
solutions.Sn1.3: Simulation results demonstrating satisfaction of sub-property 3.

The code generation process transforms t.goal: The code generation process transforms the model into the code correctly.
The code generation process transforms t.strategy: The code generation process can be decomposed into three sub-processes: model transformation, code generation, and code verification.

subgoals.G1.1: The model transformation process accurately translates the model into code.
subgoals.G1.2: The code generation process generates code from the transformed model.
subgoals.G1.3: The code verification process ensures the generated code is correct.

solutions.Sn1.1: Model-to-code mapping verification, comparison between the model and the generated code.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code generation tool verification, code quality metrics.
solutions.Sn1.3: Code review, code testing, formal verification techniques.

The synthesized code satisfies all the d.goal: The synthesized code satisfies all the desired properties with the timing tolerance.
The synthesized code satisfies all the d.strategy: The goal can be decomposed into sub-goals that verify each desired property individually and concretely define the timing tolerance.

subgoals.G1.1: Each desired property is satisfied.
subgoals.G1.2: The timing tolerance is defined precisely.

solutions.Sn1.1: Verification reports for each desired property.
solutions.Sn1.2: Timing specification documents.

Argument by use of a reliable tool set.strategy: The reliability of the software system can be determined by examining its components and their interactions.

subgoals.G1.1: The individual modules of the software system are reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The interactions between the modules are reliable.

solutions.Sn1.1: Unit testing results, code review reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Integration testing results, system logs.

Argument by harmless manual modification.strategy: The argument by harmless manual modification can be broken down into verifying the integrity of the original argument and the manual modifications made.

subgoals.G1.1: The original argument is valid and reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The manual modifications made to the argument are harmless.

solutions.Sn1.1: Reviewing the sources and evidence used in the original argument.
solutions.Sn1.2: Analyzing the impact of the manual modifications on the original argument.

The code satisfies the property PropLRI.goal: The code satisfies the property PropLRI with timing tolerance.
The code satisfies the property PropLRI.strategy: The property PropLRI can be satisfied by ensuring the code meets certain timing requirements.

subgoals.G1.1: The code meets the functional requirements of PropLRI.
subgoals.G1.2: The code meets the timing requirements of PropLRI.

solutions.Sn1.1: Functional test cases, code reviews.
solutions.Sn1.2: Timing analysis, simulation results.

The model satisfies the property PropHRI.goal: The model satisfies the property PropHRI with timing tolerance.
The model satisfies the property PropHRI.strategy: The satisfaction of PropHRI can be determined by examining the timing behavior of the model.

subgoals.G1.1: The model meets the timing requirements specified by PropHRI.
subgoals.G1.2: The model satisfies the functional requirements specified by PropHRI.

solutions.Sn1.1: Timing analysis, simulation results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Functional verification, test scenarios.

The code satisfies the property PropVRP.goal: The code satisfies the property PropVRP.
The code satisfies the property PropVRP.strategy: The code is verified and validated to ensure it satisfies the property PropVRP.

subgoals.G1.1: The code passes all verification tests for PropVRP.
subgoals.G1.2: The code passes all validation tests for PropVRP.

solutions.Sn1.1: Formal verification reports, test results from verification suite.
solutions.Sn1.2: Validation test reports, feedback from users confirming satisfaction of PropVRP.

Detect runway incursions or report failu.goal: Detect runway incursions or report failure.
Detect runway incursions or report failu.strategy: The goal of detecting runway incursions or reporting failure can be achieved by examining the various components of the system.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system can detect runway incursions.
subgoals.G1.2: The communication system can report failure.

solutions.Sn1.1: Sensor test reports, validation against known incursion scenarios.
solutions.Sn1.2: Communication system logs, failure reports from pilots or air traffic controllers.

RSM data inadequacy \ndetection is imple.goal: RSM data inadequacy detection is implemented with required dependability.
RSM data inadequacy \ndetection is imple.strategy: The adequacy of RSM data can be determined by examining its detection algorithms and the dependability measures implemented.

subgoals.G1.1: The detection algorithm is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The dependability measures meet the required standards.

solutions.Sn1.1: Algorithm performance evaluation, testing with known inadequacies.
solutions.Sn1.2: Dependability analysis, validation against dependability requirements.

Ownship runway locator reports failure w.goal: Ownship runway locator reports failure with p>=p2 if quality of information is inaccurate.
Ownship runway locator reports failure w.strategy: The failure probability of the ownship runway locator can be determined by examining the accuracy of the information it receives.

subgoals.G1.1: The ownship runway locator receives accurate information.
subgoals.G1.2: The failure probability is less than or equal to p2.

solutions.Sn1.1: Accuracy assessments of the information received by the ownship runway locator.
solutions.Sn1.2: Failure probability analysis, historical failure data.

Conflict detector reports failure with p.goal: Conflict detector reports failure with p>=p3 if quality of information is inadequate.
Conflict detector reports failure with p.strategy: The quality of information can be determined by examining its accuracy, completeness, and timeliness.

subgoals.G1.1: The information is accurate.
subgoals.G1.2: The information is complete.
subgoals.G1.3: The information is timely.

solutions.Sn1.1: Data validation reports, comparison with trusted sources.
solutions.Sn1.2: Checklists, data inventory.
solutions.Sn1.3: Timestamps, delivery records.

Traffic positions component reports fail.goal: Traffic positions component reports failure with p>=9 if quality of information is inadequate
Traffic positions component reports fail.strategy: The failure of the traffic positions component can be determined by examining the quality of the information it receives.

subgoals.G1.1: The information received by the traffic positions component is of adequate quality.

solutions.Sn1.1: Data validation reports, accuracy assessments of the information received.

Traffic positions component correctly im.goal: Traffic positions component correctly implements reasonableness checks and error detection protocol.
Traffic positions component correctly im.strategy: The correctness of the traffic positions component can be determined by examining its reasonableness checks and error detection protocol.

subgoals.G1.1: The reasonableness checks are implemented correctly.
subgoals.G1.2: The error detection protocol is implemented correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Code reviews, unit tests.
solutions.Sn1.2: Simulation results, test scenarios.

RSM data inadequacy detection is impleme.goal: RSM data inadequacy detection is implemented with required dependability.
RSM data inadequacy detection is impleme.strategy: The goal can be achieved by decomposing it into sub-goals that address different aspects of data inadequacy detection and by concretely defining the required level of dependability.

subgoals.G1.1: The system detects missing data.
subgoals.G1.2: The system detects inconsistent data.
subgoals.G1.3: The system detects incorrect data.
subgoals.G1.4: The system provides reliable and accurate detection results.

solutions.Sn1.1: Data validation algorithms, missing data detection techniques.
solutions.Sn1.2: Data consistency checks, inconsistency detection methods.
solutions.Sn1.3: Data verification techniques, error detection mechanisms.
solutions.Sn1.4: Validation against ground truth data, performance evaluation, statistical analysis.
